Title: Cotton BLH1 and KNOX6 antagonistically modulate fiber elongation via regulation of linolenic acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFAD7A-1 is a direct downstream target of GhBLH1 and is transcriptionally activated by GhBLH1 through TGGA cis-element binding in its promoter. 2. Overexpression of GhFAD7A-1 increases cotton fiber length, whereas CRISPR-Cas9 knockout shortens fibers. 3. GhFAD7A-1 promotes linolenic acid accumulation during fiber elongation and functions as a key enzymatic node in the GhBLH1/GhKNOX6 regulatory module.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA967105; metabolite profiling by GC/MS for unsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes linolenic acid accumulation and fiber elongation; knockout reduces fiber length and overexpression increases fiber length
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; fiber length phenotyping; GC/MS metabolite profiling; yeast one-hybrid; transient dual-LUC assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in rapidly elongating fibers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in rapidly elongating fibers, especially around 15 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Linolenic acid biosynthesis; unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Omega-3 desaturation branch of unsaturated fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream/terminal determinant for C18:3 formation from dienoic fatty acid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Linoleic acid (C18:2) in membrane lipid context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the omega-3 double bond to convert linoleic acid-derived substrate to linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for linolenic acid accumulation in cotton fiber elongation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants under standard farming conditions; fibers analyzed mainly at 15 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fatty acid desaturase 3/omega-3 fatty acid desaturase family
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious changes in fiber strength, micronaire, uniformity, maturity, boll shape, seed weight, or vegetative growth were detected in GhFAD7A-1 transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to increase fiber length via metabolic engineering of linolenic acid biosynthesis with limited detected penalties; may be stacked with positive regulators such as GhBLH1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic cotton
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GhFAD7A-1, but not GhFAD3-1, was directly bound by GhBLH1; GhKNOX6 indirectly suppresses its expression by interfering with GhBLH1 activity

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhBLH1 is a positive regulator of cotton fiber elongation. 2. GhBLH1 directly binds the TGGA cis-element in the GhFAD7A-1 promoter via its POX domain and activates GhFAD7A-1 transcription. 3. GhBLH1 increases linolenic acid accumulation in fibers, thereby promoting fiber elongation. 4. GhKNOX6 antagonizes GhBLH1 by direct interaction and suppresses GhBLH1-mediated activation of GhFAD7A-1.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA967105; metabolite profiling by GC/MS for unsaturated fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases fiber length and linolenic acid accumulation; RNAi decreases both
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; GC/MS metabolite profiling; yeast one-hybrid; dual-LUC transient assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; genetic interaction analysis with GhKNOX6
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GhFAD7A-1 directly; GhFAD3-1 is indirectly upregulated
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator connecting TALE transcription control to linolenic acid accumulation during fiber elongation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BEL1-LIKE HOMEODOMAIN (BLH) TALE homeodomain transcription factor with POX and HOX domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during fiber elongation, peaking at 15 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed during fiber elongation, peaking at 15 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Micronaire increased in overexpression lines and decreased in RNAi lines, but fiber strength, uniformity, maturity, boll size/shape, seed weight, and vegetative growth were largely unchanged
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for improving fiber length through activation of linolenic acid biosynthesis; should consider interaction with GhKNOX6 and micronaire effects
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage dependence, strongest effect during rapid fiber elongation around 15 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; RNAi construct for silencing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The POX domain mediates both DNA binding to the GhFAD7A-1 promoter and interaction with GhKNOX6; binds the fourth TGGA cis-element in the P2 promoter fragment

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKNOX6 is a negative regulator of cotton fiber elongation. 2. GhKNOX6 does not directly bind the GhFAD7A-1 promoter but represses GhBLH1-mediated transcriptional activation of GhFAD7A-1 by forming a heterodimer with GhBLH1. 3. Overexpression of GhKNOX6 reduces GhFAD7A-1 expression, linolenic acid accumulation, and fiber length, whereas knockout has the opposite effect.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by GC/MS for unsaturated fatty acids; genetic interaction analysis with GhBLH1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreases fiber length and linolenic acid content; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout increases both
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; fiber length phenotyping; GC/MS metabolite profiling; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; pull-down; coIP; yeast one-hybrid competition assay; dual-LUC; EMSA; genetic interaction analysis with GhBLH1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional heterodimer with GhBLH1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression indirectly via protein-protein interference with activator GhBLH1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GhBLH1 protein activity; consequently GhFAD7A-1 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-setting antagonist that restrains linolenic acid accumulation by blocking the positive regulator GhBLH1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I KNOTTED1-LIKE HOMEOBOX (KNOX) TALE homeodomain transcription factor with KNOX1, KNOX2, ELK and HOX domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in fibers at the early rapid elongation stage
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically expressed in fibers at the early rapid elongation stage
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced boll weight, flower size, number of initiating fibers, and mature seed weight; knockout slightly increased mature seed weight while not affecting vegetative growth
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for knockout/editing to improve fiber length and linolenic acid accumulation; overexpression is undesirable due to negative agronomic penalties
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage dependence during early rapid fiber elongation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The K2 domain is essential for interaction with the POX domain of GhBLH1; GhKNOX6 itself does not bind the GhFAD7A-1 promoter in yeast or EMSA
